meta.subject: English Language
meta.philosophy: The English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Level (O-Level) Secondary School has been developed to meet the needs of learners in Form 1-4. It is designed to help them develop essential skills in listening, reading, speaking, writing, creating, and reflecting upon increasingly intricate spoken, written, and multimodal texts. The aim is to foster accuracy, fluency, and purpose in communication, aligning with the Competence-Based Curriculum of the 21st Century.
meta.competence_learning: The Ordinary Level syllabus encompasses Listening and Speaking, Reading Comprehension, Composition, Structure and Summary of the English Language. Each component includes diverse topics, sub-topics, specific competences, corresponding learning activities and expected standards. Teaching follows the Communicative Language Teaching methodology prioritising both grammar rules and appropriate language use. Time allocation: Listening and Speaking (1 period/week), Reading Comprehension (2 periods/fortnight), Composition (2 periods/fortnight), Structure (1 period/week), Summary (2 periods/fortnight).
goals: Develop learners' ability to communicate effectively and confidently in English language both orally and in writing
Foster accuracy, fluency, and purpose in communication
Develop skills in listening, speaking, reading, writing, creating, and reflecting
Align with the Competence-Based Curriculum of the 21st Century
Prepare learners for further education, career opportunities, and global citizenship
Instil knowledge, skills, values, and positive attitudes to enable learners to become productive members of society

specific_outcomes: Give and receive instructions
Give and receive directions
Use appropriate language to make announcements
Use appropriate language to express sympathy
Use appropriate language to compliment others on different achievements and occasions
Use appropriate language to greet different categories of people
Use appropriate language to debate
Use appropriate language to Invite people
Use appropriate language to initiate conversations
Use appropriate language to make an offer
Use appropriate language to make requests
Use appropriate language to seek clarification
Use figures of speech in various social contexts

subtopics: 2.2.1.2 Reading Passages Efficiently and Doing Follow-up Activities
specific_outcomes: Listen to different oral sources of information and answer questions
Read passages efficiently

Write stories
Describe a place
Write a simple report
Write a speech of introduction
Write an auto- biography
Write a biography
Write an expository composition
Write an argumentative composition
Write a discursive composition
Write diary entries
Write a semi- formal letter

subtopics: 2.4.1.1 The Future Tense: The Emphantic Form of ‘shall’
specific_outcomes: Use the emphatic form of the future tense in sentences
Use the unreal past in sentences

subtopics: 2.4.2.3 Verbs followed by the Infinitive (Verb + to + Verb)
specific_outcomes: Use verbs and expressions followed by – ing in sentences
Use verbs followed by object + infinitive in sentences
Use verbs followed by the Infinitive in sentences
Use adjectives of quantity in sentences
Use possessive adjectives as determiners in sentences

subtopics: 2.4.5.1 Formation of the Comparative and Superlative
2.4.5.3 The + Comparative and the + Comparative to express parallel increase
specific_outcomes: Use comparatives and superlatives in sentences
Use alternative ways of expressing comparison
Use parallel increase to express comparison in sentences
Use other expressions to show reason in sentences
Use Participial Phrases to express reason in sentences
Use expressions of time
Use other expressions to show contrast in sentences

subtopics: 2.4.10.1 Positive, Negative, and Imperative Question Tags
specific_outcomes: Use question tags
Use the active and passive voice
Use subject- verb agreement
